column1: # Source: EBA/EIOPA/ESMA Final Report on RTS on ICT Risk Management Framework (JC 2024 29
# Scope: DORA Art. 5-9 (ICT risk management lifecycle) maps to ISO 27005 process clauses 5-10.

column9: ICT governance and risk management framework — ISO 27005 Clause 5 describes the overall risk-management process; DORA binds that to the management body for ICT resilience.
Context establishment (Clause 6) is the starting point DORA Art.5 implicitly requires for scope, criteria and stakeholders.
ICT risk management — ISO 27005 Clause 7 risk assessment covers identification, analysis and evaluation as DORA Art.6 prescribes.
Risk identification (Clause 7.2) directly supports DORA Art.6(2) — continuous identification of sources of ICT risk.
Risk analysis (Clause 7.3) implements DORA Art.6 requirement to assess likelihood and impact of ICT disruption scenarios.
Risk evaluation (Clause 7.4) aligns with DORA Art.6 requirement to compare risks against risk-acceptance criteria of the management body.
Identification of ICT supported business functions and assets — 27005 Clause 7.2 asset identification is the method DORA relies on.
Protection and prevention — ISO 27005 Clause 8 risk treatment selects and applies the controls DORA Art.8 demands (segregation, access, encryption).
Risk treatment options (8.2) — DORA Art.8 prescribes modification of risk through preventive controls.
Residual risk acceptance (8.3) — DORA requires explicit acceptance by the management body of any ICT residual risk.
Detection and monitoring — ISO 27005 Clause 10 risk monitoring and review is the process layer for DORA Art.9 continuous detection of anomalous ICT activity.
Communication and consultation (Clause 9) supports the incident-learning communications DORA Art.17 expects across organisational levels.
Context establishment covers identifying third-party risks in the ICT supply chain, which DORA Art.28 elevates to named register obligations.
Risk assessment (Clause 7) extends to third-party ICT risks — DORA Art.28 adds register-specific evidence (LEI, substitutability, subcontracting).
